Title: Host Preference Associated With Deoxynivalenol and Nivalenol Chemotypes in Fusarium graminearum, F. asiaticum, and F. meridionale

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: DON-pathway trichothecene intermediate lacking C-4 hydroxyl group
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: NIV-pathway C-4-hydroxylated trichothecene intermediate leading to NIV and 4Ac-NIV
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a hydroxyl group at C-4 in late trichothecene biosynthesis, enabling conversion toward the NIV branch instead of the DON branch.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for DON/NIV chemotype bifurcation
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Fusarium graminearum; Fusarium asiaticum; Fusarium meridionale
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Fusarium graminearum; Fusarium asiaticum; Fusarium meridionale
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Fusarium graminearum; Fusarium asiaticum; Fusarium meridionale
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Fusarium graminearum; Fusarium asiaticum; Fusarium meridionale
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native TRI13 promoter in Tri13 complementation construct
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Functional allele present in NIV chemotypes; pseudogene in DON chemotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: TRI cluster expression, including TRI13, was more strongly induced in preferred hosts during infection: higher in wheat for DON chemotype FG187 and higher in rice for NIV chemotype FA031.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TRI7; TRI1; TRI3; TRI8; TRI11; TRI101
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: infection of wheat vs rice; rice medium culture for toxin production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fungal rice culture; infected wheat heads; infected rice panicles
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fungal virulence factors that promote host infection and colonization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional TRI13 promotes NIV chemotype and rice aggressiveness, whereas loss of TRI13 shifts production toward DON chemotype and enhances wheat colonization.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: allele sequencing; gene deletion; genetic complementation with native promoter; RT-PCR/PCR verification; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; pathogenicity assays on wheat and rice
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NIV-producing strains carried a functional TRI13 allele, whereas DON chemotypes carried a nonfunctional ψTRI13 pseudogene with multiple deletions. 2. Deletion of TRI13 in NIV strains abolished NIV/4Ac-NIV production and switched toxin output to DON plus acetylated DON derivatives. 3. Complementation of DON strains with functional TRI13 generated hybrid chemotypes producing NIV together with DON and acetylated derivatives. 4. TRI13 deletion increased virulence on wheat but reduced virulence on rice, while TRI13 complementation produced the opposite host-preference shift.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: TRI13-mediated shift toward NIV reduces virulence on wheat; loss of TRI13 reduces virulence on rice.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful molecular marker for chemotype surveillance and predicting host preference/aggressiveness of Fusarium isolates; potential target for disease management diagnostics.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Complementation of 3Ac-DON and 15Ac-DON strains produced hybrid chemotypes rather than complete conversion; 3Ac-DON backgrounds showed more pronounced hybrid toxin profiles. DON strains may retain latent or alternative C-4 acetylation capacity, possibly involving residual Tri7-like activity.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Included among induced TRI cluster genes during infection; specific TRI5 host-pattern not dissected separately.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TRI3; TRI4; TRI6; TRI8; TRI10; TRI11; TRI12; TRI13; TRI14; TRI101
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: infection of wheat and rice; rice medium culture
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fungal rice culture; infected wheat heads; infected rice panicles
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fungal virulence factors promoting disease development in cereal hosts
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TRI5 is essential for DON/NIV biosynthesis and required for full virulence on both wheat and rice.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene deletion; PCR verification; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; pathogenicity assays; kernel-weight measurements
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of TRI5 in DON and NIV chemotype strains abolished detectable production of DON/NIV and their acetyl derivatives. 2. ΔTri5 mutants showed strongly reduced disease severity and AUDPC on both wheat and rice. 3. Infection by ΔTri5 mutants caused less kernel-weight loss than wild type, demonstrating the importance of trichothecenes for virulence.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of TRI5 severely reduces virulence, indicating reduced fitness in host colonization.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A robust marker/target for blocking trichothecene biosynthesis and virulence in Fusarium disease management.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used here primarily to validate that trichothecene production itself is required for pathogenicity on wheat and rice, establishing the metabolite–virulence link relevant to TRI13-mediated host preference.
